scenario: Helios Bank runs Camunda 8.8 with 6000 loans per day routed through a single deployed onboarding process. A platform operator wants to inspect 1 specific running instance to see its history and variable values, and the team also asks whether Operate itself is the right tool to analyse aggregated performance across all instances over the last 30 days.
question: How does the Inspect a process instance section instruct an operator to open instance detail and which product does it recommend for performance analysis?

options.text: Operate opens the instance detail by clicking the Process Instance Key on the Instances section under the model, showing instance history and variables; for performance the docs recommend using Optimize on top of Operate
Operate opens the instance detail by right-clicking the BPMN diagram element on the model, showing instance history and variables; for performance the docs recommend using Tasklist dashboards
Operate opens the instance detail by clicking the bpmnProcessId column on the Processes tab, showing instance history and variables; for performance the docs recommend using Console reports
Operate opens the instance detail by selecting the Operations panel entry for the instance, showing instance history and variables; for performance the docs recommend using Identity audit logs

a.text: Correct. The userguide says running process instances appear in the Instances section below the process model, and the operator clicks the Process Instance Key to open instance detail with history and variables; for visualising process-instance performance the docs recommend Optimize.

b.text: Incorrect. The instance detail is opened by clicking the Process Instance Key in the Instances section, not by interacting with the BPMN diagram; the docs recommend Optimize, not Tasklist, for performance analysis.

c.text: Incorrect. Inspect a process instance navigates from the Process Instance Key in the Instances list, not from a bpmnProcessId column; the docs recommend Optimize, not Console reports, for performance.

d.text: Incorrect. The Operations panel tracks operations such as cancellations, but the entry point for instance detail is the Process Instance Key; the recommended performance tool in the docs is Optimize, not Identity audit logs.

explanation: Inspect a process instance directs operators to the Instances section below the process model and tells them to click the Process Instance Key to open detail showing instance history and the variables attached to the instance. For visualising process-instance performance the same section recommends Optimize.
